text.title: AIO Presence Tracker Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that turn a scraper output into a trustworthy AIO presence signal.

rule.statement: The tracker MUST maintain a `control` query list of 10-20 queries with stable behaviour (known AIO presence OR known absence). Every scrape MUST verify control behaviour matches expectation; if > 30% of control queries return unexpected results, the snapshot is tagged `untrusted` and the scraper is investigated before any optimization decisions are made on the priority list.
rule.rationale: Scraper drift is the dominant failure mode in AIO tracking. Without a control list, the team mis-attributes scraper drift to content quality and rewrites content that isn't broken. Control lists are the cheapest defence.

rule.statement: Daily snapshots MUST be written exactly once per scheduled scrape. Re-running a scrape and overwriting a snapshot is FORBIDDEN. Failed scrapes are recorded with `status: error` and an `error_class` (timeout, parse_fail, captcha, 5xx) — never silently retried in place.
rule.rationale: Mutable snapshots destroy the trend signal. Errors are part of the time series — the team needs to see scraper reliability over time to make infrastructure decisions.

rule.statement: The snapshot MUST record (when AIO present): the panel position (1, 2, ...n) of our domain's citation if any, the cited snippet text verbatim, and the source title displayed. "AIO present yes/no" alone is insufficient — without position + snippet, the team cannot diagnose why citation moves.
rule.rationale: Panel position correlates with click-through-rate even within the AIO. Snippet text reveals what the model considers the answer; mismatch between intended definition and selected snippet is a high-leverage editing signal.

rule.statement: The primary KPI is `our_domain_cited_rate` (per priority query, 4-week rolling). Secondary KPIs: panel presence rate, our_snippet_selected (= verbatim from our page), our_panel_position. "AIO presence" alone is irrelevant if we are not being cited.
rule.rationale: Aggregate presence metrics decorate dashboards but do not drive decisions. The marketer cares whether the team's content is the one being cited. Other metrics are diagnostic, not primary.

rule.statement: Daily scrapes feed a weekly aggregate. The weekly aggregate is the artifact read in the Search Console review. Daily numbers are operational only; weekly numbers are decision-grade. Acting on a single daily snapshot is FORBIDDEN.

rationale.source: Statistical noise floors in SERP measurement

rule.rationale: AIO is personalised (geo, prior queries, account state). Daily noise is high; weekly aggregates suppress that noise and surface real movement.

example.title: Control-list catches scraper drift
example.code: Control query: "What is JTBD" — expected AIO present + a few stable citations. Scrape result: aio_present=false on this and 9/20 other control queries. Action: snapshot tagged `untrusted`. Adapter inspected; selector for AIO panel header changed. Adapter fix shipped; next scrape verifies.
example.note: r1 satisfied. The 30% threshold triggers; team does not waste effort rewriting priority content based on bad data.

example.title: Acting on one daily snapshot
example.code: Monday scrape: priority query "EVM software" lost citation. Action: marketer rewrites the EVM section the same afternoon. Tuesday scrape: citation back; rewrite was unnecessary.
example.note: r5 violation. Daily noise was treated as decision signal. The rewrite churned content for no measurable lift.
